title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers prefer fourth seed to avoid facing Boston Celtics in second playoff round, ESPN Cleveland host claims.
Cavaliers have exited playoffs in second round last two years, including losing to Celtics in 2024.
Boston Celtics swept Cavaliers in 2026 regular-season series and have strong recent form with 10 wins in last 12 games.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents pay the highest electricity costs in the continental U.S., varying by location and utility type.
Municipal utility customers often pay about half as much for electricity compared to those with investor-owned utilities.
Switching to municipal utilities is expensive; 225 communities use municipal aggregation to reduce costs.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students protest inadequate communication after recent violent incidents near campus, highlighting safety concerns.
Incidents include a fatal police shooting and multiple stabbings, one involving a Northeastern student near campus.
University has increased patrols but students criticize reliance on external sources for timely safety information.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: BU pauses signage policy after backlash over removal of three Pride flags from campus windows.
President Melissa Gilliam cites need for discussion after protests highlight impact on LGBTQ+ community.
The policy pause allows space for dialogue; university will seek ways to support affected groups.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Red Sox completed the Sonny Gray trade by sending pitching prospect Patrick Galle to the St. Louis Cardinals.
Patrick Galle, 22, was drafted by the Red Sox from Ole Miss and played last season with Single A Salem.
Sonny Gray, 36, is a former All-Star currently pitching for the Red Sox with a 4.50 ERA this season.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Episode 2 recap
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Episode 2 of 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' showcases tensions at Liz and Gerry McGraw's house involving Brianna's pregnancy.
Jo-Ellen Tiberi faces scrutiny about her behavior at a picnic and addresses rumors about others during a park playdate.
Rulla Nehme Pontarelli, a new cast member, deals with rumors about her husband's alleged affair, causing friction with Jo-Ellen.

title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts House to vote on bill restricting social media for children under 14 and requiring parental consent for ages 14-15.
The proposed bill mandates "bell-to-bell" cellphone bans in schools, with a 10-district pilot program planned.
Quincy is implementing an all-day cellphone ban, requiring students to secure phones in pouches during school hours.

title: Maine father dies after saving his children from a rip current
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, from North Yarmouth, died rescuing his two children from a rip current in Juno Beach, Florida.
Jennings saved his children by lifting his daughter and throwing his son toward the shore during the incident on April 1.
A GoFundMe for Jennings' family has raised over $170,000 following his death, highlighting his heroic actions.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon to support neglected and at-risk kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sean Meehan, 28, from Natick, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Home For Little Wanderers.
The Home for Little Wanderers supports children facing abuse, neglect, and trauma across the Northeast.
As America’s oldest child welfare agency, they aim to create stability and hope for at-risk kids and families.

title: The Boston Marathon didn’t make this list of best marathons in America. Do you think it should have?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Boston Marathon did not make USA Today's 2026 list of the 10 best marathons in the U.S.
The list includes the Detroit Free Press Marathon, Cincinnati Flying Pig Marathon, and Big Sur International Marathon.
The Boston Marathon draws over 30,000 runners and is part of the Abbott World Marathon Majors.
